name: Black-Out Cake
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Butter, cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F. Grease two nine-inch round cake pans and dust with flour, tapping out excess.
2. In a large bowl, beat together butter and sugar with an electric mixer or on medium speed until light and fluffy, about 1-2 minutes. Add eggs and vanilla, beating until well blended. Add chocolate and beat for another 1-2 minutes.
3. In a separate bowl, mix flour, baking soda, and salt. Add the dry ingredients to the chocolate mixture in two additions, alternately with buttermilk. Beat until well combined. With the mixer on low speed, add boiling water and beat until smooth (the batter will be thin). Pour the batter into the prepared pans.
4. Bake for 35-40 minutes, or until a cake tester inserted in the center comes out clean. Let cool in the pans for 10 minutes, then unmold onto racks and let cool completely.
5. To make Chocolate Ganache: In a 2-quart glass measure, combine chocolate chips and heavy cream. Heat in a microwave oven on HIGH for 3 minutes, or until melted and smooth when stirred. Stir in butter and vanilla.
6. Cover the pan tightly and refrigerate for 1 hour, or until the ganache holds its shape and is thick enough to spread on the cake.
7. Spread a layer of chocolate ganache over one cake layer, covering more than 1/3 of it. Place the second layer on top. Frost the top and sides of the cake with the remaining ganache. Press almonds into the side of the cake. Refrigerate the cake for 3-4 hours, or until the ganache is firm, before serving.
